translations.en: He prohibited praying at three times: sunrise, sunset, and zenith. He prohibited fasting on six days: Eid al-Fitr, the day of doubt, Eid al-Adha, and the three days of Tashriq. He prohibited sipping water the way animals drink, and said: 'Drink with your hands, for they are the best of your vessels.' He prohibited spitting in wells used for drinking. He prohibited hiring a worker without specifying his wage. He prohibited estrangement; if it must be done, one should not estrange his brother for more than three days — whoever does so longer, the Fire is most fitting for him. He prohibited selling gold and silver at a fixed price. He prohibited selling gold for gold with surplus except equal weight for equal weight. He prohibited excessive praise, and said: 'Throw dust in the faces of flatterers.' He (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'Whoever supports the argument of an oppressor, when the Angel of Death descends upon him, he will say: I give you tidings of Allah's curse and the Fire of Hell.' He said: 'Whoever praises a tyrannical ruler and humbles himself before him out of greed will be his companion in the Fire.' He (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'Allah says: Do not incline toward the oppressors lest you be touched by the Fire [11:113].'
